hypothesis: Across the 14 professionally translated MMMLU locales, o200k_base will reduce the paired token-count ratio to English versus cl100k_base in every locale and lower the median non-English inflation by at least 20 percentage points, while changing total HumanEval prompt tokens by less than 5%.
claimBoundary: The evidence supports token-count and character-proxy conclusions for pinned cl100k_base and o200k_base encodings on the declared subject-stratified MMMLU payloads and HumanEval prompts. It does not measure model quality, other providers' tokenizers, chat-template overhead, billed tokens, latency, or user traffic.

baselines: cl100k_base token counts on the same aligned payloads
controls: Aligned English source payload for every translated question
HumanEval code prompts as an out-of-domain negative control
